title: Lacosteopsis maxima
taxonomy: Lacosteopsis maxima is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It belongs to the genus Lacosteopsis, which is a small group of flowering plants. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that also includes other genera such as Apocynum and Nerium. Lacosteopsis maxima is native to Madagascar, a country located off the coast of East Africa. The species is relatively rare and is known for its unique characteristics and adaptations to the island's climate.
